---
title: "IC_R008: GitHub Issue Scope Governance Rule"
subtitle: "Create every issue in global_scripts and classify with scope labels"
chapter: "CH06"
category: "rule"
number: "IC_R008"
date-created: "2026-02-28"
date-modified: "2026-02-28"
author: "Claude"
type: "rule"
law: "Integration & Collaboration Law Implementation Rules"
article-number: "Article 8"
implements:
  - "CH00.MP012: Change Tracking Principle"
related_to:
  - "SO_R029: CHANGELOG Organization Rule"
  - "IC_R007: GitHub Issue Attachment Workflow"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article Overview {#overview}

**Integration & Collaboration Law Implementation Rules Article 8 (GitHub Issue Scope Governance Rule)**

This article requires that all issue reporting use GitHub Issues in the `ai_martech_global_scripts` repository as the only source of truth, and that each issue is labeled with correct scope for routing and board mapping.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Rule
Every actionable issue must be created in `kiki830621/ai_martech_global_scripts` and labeled with `company:<COMPANY_CODE>` (preferred) or at minimum `module:<ModuleName>` when company scope is intentionally unknown.
:::

# Section 1: Mandatory Source of Truth {#source-of-truth}

## 1.1 Issue Repository

- Primary issue tracker: `https://github.com/kiki830621/ai_martech_global_scripts/issues`
- Local `changelog/issues` directories are historical/legacy only and are not used for active tracking.
- GitHub Issue number is required for follow-up implementation reports.

## 1.2 Mandatory Labeling

### Company scope (preferred)

- Use exactly one company scope label for company-owned issues:
  - `company:MAMBA`
  - `company:QEF_DESIGN`
  - etc.

### Module scope (allowed)

- If scope is module-specific or cross-company:
  - `module:<ModuleName>` (e.g., `module:BrandEdge`)

### Forbidden labels

- Do **not** use `project:*`, `app:*`, or uppercase `Company:*` labels for issue routing.

# Section 2: Execution Workflow {#workflow}

## 2.1 Required Issue Workflow

```bash
gh issue create \
  --repo kiki830621/ai_martech_global_scripts \
  --title "<clear title>" \
  --body-file issue_body.md \
  --label "bug" \
  --label "priority:P1" \
  --label "company:<COMPANY_CODE>"
```

## 2.2 Issue Body Minimum

New issue descriptions should include:

- **Problem**
- **Expected behavior**
- **Actual behavior**
- **Impact**
- **Suggested owner or impacted app/company**

# Section 3: Board Linkage {#board-linkage}

After creation, add issue to the matching board based on scope labels:

- `company:*` => company board
- `module:*` => module board

If scope is unclear, put into triage board first and relabel when clarified.

# Section 4: Local Documentation Policy {#local-docs}

Implementation of an issue should be documented in local reports:

- `scripts/global_scripts/00_principles/changelog/reports/YYYY-MM-DD_issue-<number>_<short-title>.md`

The report must include:

- GitHub issue URL
- Commit/PR link
- Scope labels used
- Summary of impact and status

# Section 5: Rationale {#rationale}

This rule exists to:

- Keep issue tracking single-sourced and queryable.
- Keep cross-repo scope separation clear across companies and modules.
- Ensure downstream boards receive issues predictably.
- Preserve historical reporting and implementation traceability.
